records.title: Use LaunchAgent instead of Cron for scheduled tasks
records.summary: LaunchAgent selected over cron for all scheduled automation on macOS.
records.decision: LaunchAgent is the standard macOS scheduling mechanism and provides better integration with the OS.
records.reason: Survives reboot, better logging via stdout/stderr, KeepAlive support, can be managed with launchctl.
records.author: monkey

records.title: Filter buttons must use URL search params, not React state
records.summary: Task/Incident filter buttons broke silently because onClick+useState depends on React hydration.
records.problem: Clicking filter buttons (Operational, Feature, Open, Critical) did nothing. No error, no visual feedback.
records.rootCause: Filter state used useState initialized from default. If React hydration failed silently (stale bundle, HMR issue), onClick handlers never attached and state updates were no-ops.
records.fix: Converted filter buttons from <button onClick={setFilterMode}> to <Link search={{ view: mode }}>. Filter state derived from URL via useSearch(). Works without JavaScript hydration.
records.neverDoThis: Never rely on React useState + onClick for critical navigation/filter UI. Use URL params as source of truth.
records.author: monkey

records.title: node:fs static imports in server-data barrel export crash all pages
records.summary: When predictions.ts and performance.ts were accidentally barrel-exported, all pages returned 500.
records.problem: Every page on Mission Control returned HTTP 500. No specific error visible to the user.
records.rootCause: predictions.ts and performance.ts use static `import { readFileSync } from 'node:fs'` at top level. When re-exported through the barrel (index.ts), Vite's bundler resolved the entire module chain for client components, pulling server-only code into the client bundle.
records.Fix: Never barrel-export files with Node.js built-in imports. Import directly from the module file (e.g. @/lib/server-data/predictions).
records.neverDoThis: Before adding any file to barrel export, check for Node.js built-in imports. If present, import directly instead.
records.author: monkey

records.title: Corrupted file write causes 500 on all pages
records.summary: A corrupted performance.ts (encoding issue during write) broke every page on the site.
records.problem: All pages returned HTTP 500 after a file edit. The Vite dev server showed PARSE_ERROR with garbled characters.
records.rootCause: File write introduced encoding corruption (√ character at import statement). The file was syntactically invalid JavaScript.
records.fix: Rewrote the file with correct encoding. Verified with head command before restarting.
records.neverDoThis: Always verify file integrity after writes (head -5, grep for expected patterns). A single corrupted server-data file takes down the entire site.
records.author: monkey

records.title: FreeRide watcher disabled due to rate limit exhaustion
records.summary: The freeride-watcher LaunchAgent was disabled because it was burning rate limit quota without providing value.
records.decision: FreeRide watcher disabled. Models checked manually on demand.
records.reason: When the free model pool is exhausted, each poll generates 429 errors. Exponential backoff was added but the watcher was still counterproductive during dry spells.
records.alternativeConsidered: Keep running with exponential backoff — rejected because it still burns quota.
records.author: monkey

records.title: Do not use Date.now() in SSR components
records.summary: Date.now() produces different values on server and client, causing hydration mismatches.
records.rule: Never use Date.now(), new Date(), or any time-dependent function directly in SSR component render paths. Use UTC timestamps from data, or render time-dependent content only on the client.
records.reason: SSR renders on the server at one time, client hydrates at a different time. Mismatched timestamps cause React hydration errors.
records.examples: Use toLocaleTimeString in useEffect or client-only components. Use UTC dates from data files for SSR.
records.author: monkey

records.title: Commit after every meaningful change
records.summary: Every code change should be committed individually with a clear message.
records.rule: After any meaningful change (routes, components, styles, assets) run git status, commit with a clear message, and push to GitHub. This is enforced before starting new work.
records.reason: Atomic commits make it easy to identify what changed, roll back specific changes, and maintain a clear history.
records.author: monkey

records.title: WD MyCloud SMB share not mounted
records.summary: The WD MyCloud backup target at /Volumes/Public is not mounted. Backup scripts exist but cannot run.
records.issue: WD MyCloud SMB share is not mounted at /Volumes/Public. All backup scripts (openclaw-backup-quick.sh, openclaw-backup.sh, safe-backup.sh) check for the mount and fail gracefully.
records.currentState: Backups are not running. Time Machine backup IS mounted at a different path but that is Apple's backup, not ours.
records.workaround: Mount the share via Finder or mount_smbfs when backups are needed.
records.author: monkey
